tokens: I wonder where we stand on time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Looking at his watch) Well, we've been out about 5 hours and 20 minutes or so.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Where are we headed, now that we are moving?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'm trying to get out of the block field here, then I'll head back to the southwest.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We going to Sherlock (Station 10) at all, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) That must be Gatsby (Crater) over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...you can follow the (garbled, but means the Nav system) home.
We can get lots of photos.
We've got lots of film left right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, I guess you're the one that took the SEP out.
Do you remember the reading of the SEP temperature when you broke it down?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It was 125 when we started the station.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's Gatsby there, I guess, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's not unlike Van Serg, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, you know that looks like the mantling...Hopefully, we can get a...Watch your rock.
(Hopefully), we can get a shot looking back to the northwest...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'll get that when I...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...into Gatsby, because it looks like the mantle streams over the side from the southwest.
Can you swing to your right and get up a little closer to the rim, there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, there is a couple fragments in spots...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, what I'm looking at is the northwest portion of Gatsby, where there's a very, very concentrated block field on the inner wall; (that is, we can see blocks) except where there are, on the southwest, three streams and, on the northwest and north, a continuous stream - if you will - or radial band of mantle that appears to be burying that field (or) overlying and mantling the field.
We got some pretty good pictures of it, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I'm more and more convinced there's a mantle.

(Pause) One possibility, I guess, is that, if it's a pyroclastic mantle, that in the lunar vacuum environment and with whatever volatiles we're dealing with, the stuff becomes extremely fine upon vesiculation.
We may have been on it all the time and not known it...(that is), as far as recognizing it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See, as soon as we come through this draw, how smooth or free of any debris or boulders it is on the other side of the upslope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bob, do we have an extra EP (Explosive Package)?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We have two of them behind you.
I'll give you a reading soon on that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, one I deploy at the end, I know, (near the place where he will park the Rover at the end of the EVA).
I thought we had an extra one here somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's the one we were planning on deploying all along, and it's there.
We'll be deploying at a range of 0.1, which is just before you get to the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) I guess Sherlock's going to be right over the top over here.
I saw it when we were on that other ridge.
(Pause) Hey, you know, there's a lot of bad landing places around here.
That low Sun angle, I think, shows most of them up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...and if you keep going straight to the LM, you're probably going to run into this crater area around San Luis Rey.
You probably ought to head somewhat south of directly back to the LM, so we can at least tip the western edge of Sherlock and then pick it up and go from there back to the SEP.
It looks like it might be rather rough there in that dotted-lined area, if you can look at the backside of your map, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I've already been doing it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Bob, about 200 meters back, we crossed back into our standard mantle surface of about one percent fragment cover...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...(when we came) out of the block field, which...(Stops to listen; pause) (To Gene) I can see the LM.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I can see the LM.
And there's Sherlock, where those blocks are.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's the block field, the Sherlock block field; that's right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I might even call it "10 Alpha" in honor of the Apollo Program Office.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I knew I'd never get that straight!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do those blocks look like gabbros to you guys?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How fast do you think we're going, Jack, without looking?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we're going about 18 clicks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Seems like the first time we've been able to go downhill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Pull close to this big block, if you can over here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I'll try to get a reading on what it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Some pictures of it as we come up to it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like our old friend, the subfloor...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Vesicles are about a centimeter maximum size.
They look like they're fairly evenly sorted.
And the rock itself seemed to be massive.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we're back into about a five percent rock cover as we cross the edge of the Sherlock block field.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's Sherlock over that rim over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Once again, all these subfloor blocks look as if they're buried.
Not mantled, necessarily, except maybe that one.
Can you swing right, just a tad?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That one's got the mantle blowing up on it, in it...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's the best example of that, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, do those blocks look like the same subfloor gabbro?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, that's just got the mantle...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Everything in here, so far, is the tan-gray subfloor gabbro that I've seen.
I haven't...Oh, there's one over there that's the blue-gray.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) And, 17, as you're getting closer, we're going to want an LRV sample at 1.1 (km) on the range.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We'll try to get block and soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Bob, I am continually impressed by the lack of exotic fragments in here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about picking out a place...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If you head into that little...Well, that's a crater there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can go a little bit further.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll go up on that flat area up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) There are a lots of little fragments over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you get any of those?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Unfortunately, I can't see them (now, because they're in) the shadow (of the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about that one right in front of you, in front of the television camera shadow.
See that little one up there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: With the...It's a little big, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you can get over there, I can get it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I guess I had the wrong...I guess I wasn't looking at the right one.
The (Rover) shadow is making it impossible to see down there.
Now, see what you can get.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're going to have to...If we do another sample, you're going to have to swing right so I can see.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't see to get a rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Go forward just a little bit, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Except you're going to get yourself in a box here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't see the LM anymore.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got a rock right in front of you, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: LMP frame for that sample (pause) looks like about six-zero.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Have I taken sixty pictures (since leaving Van Serg)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, these rock fields are something else again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like some of our gray variety of subfloor up here, around the rim of that little crater.

You know, I'm starting to think that maybe the gray, relatively-nonvesicular subfloor may be (the) deeper fraction, based on what we saw...Well, actually, though...Let's see, that could have been overturned.
There just isn't much of it around here, although we saw a lot of it in the wall of Cochise.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What do you think this is, San Luis Rey?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wouldn't doubt it at all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll bet that's San Luis Rey.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're on the east side of it...Mariner and San Luis Rey.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As close as we can tell, you're at one or the other of them.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, I tell you they're a lot...(To Bob) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) Boy, I certainly don't see much variety other than the gray and the tan subfloor varieties.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, I tell you, there's no getting out of this stuff (the boulder fields).
You go from one to the other.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know whether I said it or not...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're going to deploy the quarter-pound charge, and that'll be Jack's getting off to deploy it like we talked about last night.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Bob, we're moving in and out of areas of, say, 1 percent to 5 -to-10 percent blockiness.
And where it gets blocky, not only is it more blocky, but we seem to have more of the medium-sized craters in the range of 20- to 50-meter-diameter craters.
(To Gene) That may be Mariner right there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Hey...Van Serg, let me mention again, was an unusual experience in the plains geology here.
(To Gene) That must be part of San Luis Rey or Mariner, one of them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The crater on our left - that is, south of us - is a large crater.
It's somewhat deeper than (other) craters of the same size (diameter) that we've seen.
And it, too, though, has large blocks mainly in the walls, although there are blocks up here in the rim, occasionally up to 3 meters (in size).
(Pause) Look at that string of blocks over there; that may be it (that is, San Luis Rey).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's an edge of a crater, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look at the way that thing's fractured.
Yeah, this is the San Luis Rey complex, because see how elongated it is?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Fact is, we're going to cut right through the western half here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I may have said early on - up there at Van Serg - that I saw subfloor, but we never did sample any that I know of.
And the dust was thick enough that I'm just not sure.
The breccias were the most obvious thing there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It might have been a window in the plains here, of some kind.
But it's strange to see it there, with so much subfloor all around it that we saw.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, you're going to get a feedwater tone pretty soon.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause as he switches to the Aux supply) Okay, I should be in Aux(illary) now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And as soon as we get to the 0.1 (range), let's stop and deploy the charge.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Then we'll head back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Unless you want to go to the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When do I go to the...Oh, you go to SEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I go to SEP when I...Take the Rover around here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're going to let you play the Return-to-the-ALSEP game there, Jack.
We've got a few things for you to do out there, when the time comes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It'll be just short of SEP.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Is it short of the antenna?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...You see, we can get to the end of the antenna...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's have it east of the antenna.
If we are there, let's deploy it right where you are.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're about 30 meters east of the antenna.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a rock (that) I stood up down there, and I want to get...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it's EP number 2 that we're after, Jack, in case you didn't follow us.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...It's the only dense (that is, fine grained) rock (I've seen)...(Stops to listen to Bob) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) But it looks like everything's here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is that the gate or the pallet?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Seems to ride all right that way.
(Pause) Boy, that dust is getting into everything.
(Pause) Okay, I'm going to leave the gate like it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're just going to walk back from here, aren't you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, why don't you just go turn the SEP receiver off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sure, the transmitter...But you've got to come out here anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I've got to come out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I was just reading ahead, but no sense (in changing).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) And I'll try to put it in a depression.
(To Bob) I'm going to put it in a depression, if you want.
(Looking at LMP-29) And then I've got to take a pan, huh?
How about a "locator" to the LM?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You going to get on, Jack, or walk back?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll give you a frame count, if I can read it: (Pause) 90.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) You're going to have to go left a little, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we don't have to worry about it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...we don't have to worry about it, but I will anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Now on the Rover, probably without his seatbelt on) Go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I want to point out a rock to you I set up on end (that) we need to get in a bag.
You can let me off there and I'll carry it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But drive close enough so I can reach down and use the Rover for support.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: On which side of that antenna?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, it's out...It's near the LM.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I can go across this thing (a SEP antenna wire).
(Noticing a sample bag lying on the surface ahead of them) That bag is empty, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Voice quality altered because he's got his chin down) Look's like you got over (the antenna wire).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I think it's that one there that's sort of dark.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you swing over so I can lean on the Rover when I pick it up?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I am now...I'd hate to get run over this late in the game.
(Pause) Well, now, what did I do that for?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Kneeling beside the Rover) Need your oil changed?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: While you're under there, (laughing) would you check my transmission, please?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) Any bubbles on the inside of the tires?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's halfway between the SEP and the LM.
(To Gene, who has started to drive away) Wait, wait!
Let me put it in the big bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is this that brown one you saw out here before, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Answering Bob) No, it's a gray one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I just lost a sample.
(Pause) It's in my pocket, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt
